item.title: datasette code-frequency chart on GitHub 摘要
item.description:
Simon Willison explores the impact of coding agents and advanced AI models on his productivity by analyzing the GitHub code frequency chart for his Datasette project. The chart shows sporadic bursts of activity from 2018 to 2026, with the largest spike in 2026 (37,022 additions, -9,528 deletions) coinciding with models like Opus 4.8, GPT-5.5, Fable 5, and GPT-5.6 Sol. This suggests that AI-assisted programming significantly boosts code output, especially in later years.
标签: datasette, github, ai, generative-ai, llms, ai-assisted-programming, coding-agents, productivity, open-source, code-frequency

item.title: DOOMQL 摘要
item.description:
DOOMQL is a Doom-like game built by Peter Gostev using GPT-5.6 Sol, where SQLite serves as the game engine for movement, collision, enemies, combat, and rendering. It runs as a Python terminal script and uses a recursive CTE to implement a full ray tracer in SQL. Simon Willison extended it by creating a Datasette App that displays the game screen and a tactical map, refreshing every second via SQL queries on the underlying SQLite database.
标签: games, sql, sqlite, ai, datasette, generative-ai, llms, ai-assisted-programming, gpt, datasette-apps
